title: Device Compliance Status Overview

compliance.assurance_rationale: Monitors device configuration compliance status against Catalyst Center policies, providing evidence of configuration baseline enforcement.
compliance.controlObjective: Provides continuous visibility into how many devices meet Catalyst Center compliance policies versus how many are out of policy for NIST 800-53 CM-6 (configuration settings).
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search `UC-5.13.28` over `index=catalyst` and `cisco:dnac:compliance` (scheduled daily), exported to the compliance evidence dashboard; alert optional when NON_COMPLIANT share exceeds the agreed threshold.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Demonstrates network device configuration compliance monitoring as part of firewall and router configuration standards.
compliance.controlObjective: Contributes to PCI DSS 1.1.1 by showing network security control configuration is monitored against an enforced baseline in Catalyst Center.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Same saved search and dashboard as the primary UC artefact, with a monthly PDF or CSV compliance snapshot for the CDE/network boundary device population covered by the feed.

dataSources: index=catalyst, sourcetype cisco:dnac:compliance (Catalyst Center /dna/intent/api/v2/compliance/detail; fields complianceStatus, deviceName, complianceType)
spl: index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:compliance" | stats count by complianceStatus | eventstats sum(count) as _total | eval pct=round(100*count/_total,1)
description: Provides an overview of device compliance status across the managed infrastructure as reported by Catalyst Center compliance policies.
value: Compliance is a continuous requirement. This overview shows at a glance how many devices are compliant, non-compliant, or in error state.
implementation: Enable the `compliance` input in the Cisco Catalyst TA. The TA polls `GET /dna/intent/api/v2/compliance/detail` on a **900s** default interval. Key fields: `complianceStatus` (COMPLIANT, NON_COMPLIANT, ERROR, IN_PROGRESS, NOT_APPLICABLE), `deviceName`, `complianceType` (RUNNING_CONFIG, IMAGE, PSIRT, EOX, NETWORK_SETTINGS).
visualization: Pie or donut (count by complianceStatus), single value (compliant %), table with status and percentage.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
ÔÇó Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 7538) installed; **compliance** feed landing in `index=catalyst` with sourcetype `cisco:dnac:compliance`.
ÔÇó Catalyst Center **2.2+** (confirm **Intent API v2** in your build); service account with **`NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE`** or higher read access to **Compliance** APIs (exact role names may vary by versionÔÇöverify in ISE/AAA for the API user).
ÔÇó Retention and export policy aligned to **NIST/PCI** evidence needs (often **90ÔÇô365 days** in the `catalyst` index or a **summary** index for compliance snapshots).
ÔÇó See `docs/implementation-guide.md` for `inputs.conf`, secure credential storage, and index sizing.

Step 1 ÔÇö Configure data collection
ÔÇó **Intent API:** `GET /dna/intent/api/v2/compliance/detail`.
ÔÇó **TA input name:** **compliance**; sourcetype `cisco:dnac:compliance`.
ÔÇó **Default interval:** **900 seconds (15 minutes)**ÔÇöbalance API load vs freshness; shorten only if your change-approval process requires it.
ÔÇó **Key fields:** `complianceStatus` (**COMPLIANT**, **NON_COMPLIANT**, **ERROR**, **IN_PROGRESS**, **NOT_APPLICABLE**), `deviceName`, `complianceType` (**RUNNING_CONFIG**, **IMAGE**, **PSIRT**, **EOX**, **NETWORK_SETTINGS**).
ÔÇó **Volume note:** the TA may emit **one or many events** per pollÔÇöuse **dedup**/`latest` in downstream panels if you see duplicate **deviceName** + **complianceType** rows per bucket.

Step 2 ÔÇö Create the search and report
```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:compliance" | stats count by complianceStatus | eventstats sum(count) as _total | eval pct=round(100*count/_total,1)
```

Understanding this SPL
ÔÇó **`stats` + `eventstats`** yields a **stable percentage** of each `complianceStatus` value for the selected window (prefer **`eventstats`** over `sum(count)` in **`eval`** alone for clarity at scale).
ÔÇó Filter with `complianceType=<value>` in a **post-process** or **base + append** if you need **separate** pies per policy family (for example **IMAGE** vs **RUNNING_CONFIG**).

**Pipeline walkthrough**
ÔÇó Scopes **`cisco:dnac:compliance`**; rolls up **counts** and attaches **%** of total for dashboard tiles and audit tables.

Step 3 ÔÇö Validate
ÔÇó **Compare the percentage of devices in each status (especially COMPLIANT vs NON_COMPLIANT) against the Catalyst Center Compliance dashboard** for the same scope and timeÔÇöSplunk and the UI should agree within one **poll**.
ÔÇó Spot-check a few **deviceName** values that appear as **ERROR** or **IN_PROGRESS** in Splunk and confirm the same in the GUI.
ÔÇó Run **`| timechart count by complianceStatus`** to ensure **continuous** ingestion; long gaps indicate TA or API issues, not a silent ÔÇťall greenÔÇŁ network.

Step 4 ÔÇö Operationalize
ÔÇó **Dashboard:** donut or bar by **complianceStatus**, **single value** for **% COMPLIANT**, plus a **table** of top exceptions when filtered to **NON_COMPLIANT**.
ÔÇó **Evidence:** schedule this saved search **daily** and archive CSV/PDF to your GRC or evidence store per your control framework.
ÔÇó **IN_PROGRESS spikes** often follow **bulk template pushes**ÔÇöannotate change windows on the panel so teams do not misread a temporary state.

Step 5 ÔÇö Troubleshooting
ÔÇó **No events:** enable **compliance** input, re-check **Catalyst** base URL, token, and role; search **`splunkd.log`** for **HTTP 401/403/429**.
ÔÇó **Partial statuses only:** your policies may be **unassigned** for some platformsÔÇö**NOT_APPLICABLE** dominance is a **Catalyst** configuration topic, not a Splunk parse bug.
ÔÇó **Count mismatch vs UI:** align **time zone**, **device inventory scope** (virtual domain / site), and **dedup** logic with what the operator filters in the **Compliance** UI.
ÔÇó **Stuck IN_PROGRESS:** confirm **on-device** or **Catalyst** jobs are not **stalled**; open **TAC** if compliance jobs **never** complete for a class of devices.
grandmaExplanation: We show you how many of your network devices meet the required configuration standards and how many do not.
